title: Test coverage | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# Test coverage

## [General guidelines](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/overview/#general-guidelines "General guidelines")

To begin with, you should already have set up CI-based analysis. That is, you should have the appropriate SonarScanner configured to perform code analysis as part of your build pipeline.

To enable coverage reporting, you must then do the following:

1. Set up your coverage tool to run as part of your build pipeline *before* the SonarScanner analysis step.

2. Configure the coverage tool so that the location and format of the output report files match what the SonarScanner expects.

3. Configure the analysis parameters of the SonarScanner so that it can import the report files.

Now, on each build of your project, your coverage tool should perform its analysis and output its results to one or more files (usually one for test coverage and one for test execution). Then, the SonarScanner, as part of its analysis process, should import those files and send the results to SonarQube Cloud.

## [Coverage support](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/overview/#coverage-support "Coverage support")

SonarQube Cloud directly supports the import of coverage data in formats native to a variety of tools for a variety of languages. It also supports the import of a generic format that can be used as a target for custom conversion of reports from tools that are not directly supported.

### [Detailed guides](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/overview/#detailed-guides "Detailed guides")

Detailed guides for the following languages are provided in this section:

- [Dart test coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/dart-test-coverage/ "Dart test coverage")
- [Java Test Coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/java-test-coverage/ "Java Test Coverage")
- [JavaScript/TypeScript Test Coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/javascript-typescript-test-coverage/ "JavaScript/TypeScript Test Coverage")
- [.NET Test Coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/dotnet-test-coverage/ ".NET Test Coverage")
- [Python Test Coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/python-test-coverage/ "Python Test Coverage")
- [PHP Test Coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/php-test-coverage/ "PHP Test Coverage")
- [C/C++/Objective-C Test Coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/c-c-objective-c-test-coverage/ "C/C++/Objective-C Test Coverage")

### [Generic format](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/overview/#generic-format "Generic format")

See [Generic test data](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/generic-test-data/ "Generic test data") for information on how the generic format works.

### [Test coverage parameters](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/overview/#test-coverage-parameters "Test coverage parameters")

See [Test coverage parameters](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/test-coverage-parameters/ "Test coverage parameters") for a reference on all coverage-related analysis parameters.

## [Test execution reports](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/overview/#test-execution-reports "Test execution reports")

This section is about *test coverage reports*, that is, reports that describe the percentage of your code that is tested by your test suite during a build.

Test execution reports are a separate feature. These describe which tests within your tests suite are executed during a build. For details, see [Test Execution Parameters](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/enriching/test-coverage/test-execution-parameters/ "Test Execution Parameters").
